stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces MetaBackdoor, a novel backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
Challenges the assumption that LLM backdoors require explicit content-based triggers.
Exploits positional encoding within LLMs as a covert and powerful attack surface.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and DeepSeek architecture.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is rolling out its AI likeness detection tool to all adult users (18+).
The feature utilizes a selfie-style scan to monitor the platform for potential deepfakes of the user.
If a match is found, the user is alerted by the platform.

stories.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: ArXiv is implementing new policies to combat the submission of low-quality research papers.
The platform will ban authors whose papers contain 'AI slop,' defined by unchecked LLM generation.
Specific violations include hallucinated references or detectable 'meta-comments' from AI tools.

stories.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI is undergoing another executive reorganization to focus on its product strategy.
The company is consolidating products to heavily invest in the development of AI agents.
Greg Brockman has been designated the official lead for all product-related initiatives at OpenAI.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively deploying AI to combat insider trading.
The focus of this AI application is specifically within the realm of prediction markets.
This signals a significant regulatory shift towards using advanced machine learning for financial market integrity.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The approval of this historic settlement is facing delays from the judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers rushed the settlement to secure $320 million in fees.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs with new, domain-specific knowledge.
The core innovation is encoding new knowledge into a dedicated memory model without altering the original LLM parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, making them more adaptable to real-world, timely data.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs with new knowledge without retraining the core model.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, which hinders real-world, timely application.
Achieves knowledge incorporation by encoding new information into a dedicated memory model.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Identifies a systematic dual failure where gradient-based unlearning methods lose effectiveness under 4-bit compression, while quantization-robust methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.
Proposes 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning' as a necessary advancement to ensure model forgetting persists in deployed, compressed models.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in STRIDE, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The research synthesizes findings from 932 arXiv security studies (2023-2026) to validate threat surface coverage.

stories.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
stories.author: gemini-enterprise
bullet_points: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available.
It is being deployed on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.
The model is highlighted for being fast and cost-efficient.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The focus is on a specific model's trending status within the ML community.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tools and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: US, China, and Russia are actively increasing their surveillance and presence in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO).
The article suggests that satellites are not as stealthy as previously assumed, making them visible against the blackness of space.
The content points to a growing geopolitical competition in the space domain.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge of maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) over long video sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, providing a more rigorous evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational challenge of visual reasoning, comparing direct image generation versus alternative methods.
It contrasts two main approaches: agentic reasoning (using code/tools) and latent reasoning (using hidden embeddings).
A key drawback of agentic methods noted is the latency introduced by external execution and context switching.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agent adaptability in dynamic environments.
The system works by chronologically replaying real-world events (like news articles) to ground agent interactions.
Allows agents to forecast future world events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with historical context.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a feed-forward native 3D scene editing method.
It addresses the limitation of current 3D reconstruction models in handling dynamic, interactive human instructions.
The method moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies used in existing scene editing techniques.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D geometric consistency of generative video models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for this evaluation.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide objective diagnostics for geometric failures.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
The problem arises from co-training disparate Partial Differential Equation (PDE) regimes, causing gradient conflict in dense models.
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands from different physics simulations.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces OpenDeepThink, a new framework for improving LLM reasoning by scaling computation breadth.
Addresses the selection bottleneck in parallel reasoning by using pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation instead of relying on noisy pointwise judging.
Focuses on test-time compute scaling, a critical area for advancing Large Language Model (LLM) capabilities.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical images.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve screening accuracy and transparency.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (precise timing).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative richness and temporal accuracy.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex safety claims demanded by current AI governance frameworks.
AI governance requires evidence of properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited to observable model outputs, creating an epistemological gap.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a known architectural asymmetry in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional.
Proposes RefDecoder, a method to enhance visual generation by applying equal conditioning to the decoder.
Aims to improve structural integrity and reduce detail loss/inconsistency in video generation tasks.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world scenarios.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically to allow agents to adapt to evolving information.
Agents can forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with this historical replay.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing neural network components.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries.
Crucial for mechanistic interpretability, allowing researchers to verify if different model parts implement the same computation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and optimization instability when co-training diverse PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows).
Proposes a solution, 'Shod' (Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing), to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands in dense neural operators.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical imaging.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide more transparent reasoning pathways for clinical diagnosis.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment for this reconstruction, relevant to complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper argues that current behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex AI safety claims required by governance frameworks.
Key unverified properties include the absence of hidden objectives, resistance to loss-of-control, and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations and red-teaming) are limited to observable model outputs, creating an epistemological gap.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The research addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models when performing dexterous manipulation.
A key challenge identified is the command mismatch during human takeover in Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) for high-DoF robotic hands.
The proposed 'Hand-in-the-Loop' method aims to improve VLA performance by enabling seamless interventional correction.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It proposes On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new grounded simulation framework for testing AI agents.
The simulation replays real-world events chronologically to mimic dynamic, open-ended environments.
It allows agents to forecast future events beyond their knowledge cutoff while interacting with historical data.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper addresses the interaction between retrieval strategy, agent architecture, and tool-calling in LLM-based agentic search.
It highlights the current gap in literature regarding systematic comparisons of these components within RAG-enhanced agentic systems.
The research focuses on practical dimensions of complex, autonomous LLM agent workflows.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes a multimodal approach combining unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (precise timing).
The method utilizes Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the temporal precision gap between text and tables.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses the gap where current evaluations often rely only on textual traces, ignoring fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to preserve visual evidence necessary for complex, state-changing reasoning.

stories.title: Δ-Mem: Efficient Online Memory for Large Language Models
stories.author: 44za12
bullet_points: The article introduces 'Δ-Mem,' a new system designed for efficient online memory management.
This technology is specifically targeted at addressing memory constraints in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It represents a technical advancement in making LLMs more practical for large-scale, continuous operations.
